text: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower in Paris, France. It was completed in 1889 and stands 330 metres tall. It was designed by the engineer Gustave Eiffel.
Mount Everest is Earth's highest mountain above sea level, located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and Tibet. Its peak reaches 8849 metres.
Python is a high-level programming language created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. It emphasizes readable code and is widely used for data science and machine learning.
The transformer is a neural network architecture introduced in the 2017 paper Attention Is All You Need. It relies on self-attention and underpins large language models.
Retrieval-augmented generation combines a retriever that fetches relevant documents with a generator that conditions its answer on them, reducing hallucination on knowledge-intensive tasks.
The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system, located off the coast of Queensland, Australia. It stretches over 2300 kilometres and is visible from space.
Water is a chemical compound with the formula H2O. Each molecule contains two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom. It freezes at zero degrees Celsius.
The Apollo 11 mission landed the first humans on the Moon in July 1969. Neil Armstrong was the first person to walk on the lunar surface, followed by Buzz Aldrin.
BM25 is a bag-of-words ranking function used by search engines to score documents by term frequency and inverse document frequency. It is a strong sparse retrieval baseline.
The Amazon River in South America is the largest river by discharge volume of water in the world, and by most measures the second longest after the Nile. It flows through Brazil.
Gustave Eiffel was a French civil engineer. Besides the Eiffel Tower in Paris, he designed the internal frame of the Statue of Liberty in New York.
A cross-encoder reranker jointly encodes a query and a candidate passage to produce a relevance score. It is more accurate but slower than dense bi-encoder retrieval.
